prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political pressure group led by former Democrat Party parliamentary representative Suthep Thaugsuban, organised anti-government protests in Thailand from November 2013 to May 2014. These protests resulted in the removal of incumbent Prime Minister Yingluck Shinawatra, a coup, and the establishment of a military junta. The primary objective of the protests was to remove former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra and the establishment of an unelected "people's council" in Thailand.
B. The 2013–2014 Thai political crisis was a period of political instability in Thailand. Anti-government protests took place between November 2013 and May 2014, organised by the People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political pressure group led by former Democrat Party parliamentary representative (MP) Suthep Thaugsuban. The crisis eventually resulted in the removal of incumbent Prime Minister Yingluck Shinawatra, a coup d'état, and the establishment of a military junta. The primary aim of the protests was the removal of former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra's – the brother of Yingluck – influence on Thai politics and the creation of an unelected "people's council" to oversee reforms of the political system. Protesters viewed Thaksin as corrupt and damaging to Thailand's democracy, although he enjoyed strong support in many areas of Thailand,
C. A phase of political upheaval in Thailand, known as the 2013–2014 Thai political crisis, transpired during this time. Demonstrations against the government were organized by the People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political advocacy group spearheaded by Suthep Thaugsuban, a former Member of Parliament (MP) for the Democrat Party, and occurred between November 2013 and May 2014. This crisis ultimately culminated in the ousting of the then-current Prime Minister, Yingluck Shinawatra, a military takeover, and the subsequent formation of a military government. The main objective of these protests was to eradicate the influence of former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra – who was Yingluck's brother – from Thai politics and to establish an appointed "people's council" that would supervise modifications to the political framework. Despite enjoying considerable backing in numerous regions of Thailand, protesters perceived Thaksin as dishonest and detrimental to the democratic process in Thailand.

Answer:

options.A: The People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political pressure group led by former Democrat Party parliamentary representative Suthep Thaugsuban, organised anti-government protests in Thailand from November 2013 to May 2014. These protests resulted in the removal of incumbent Prime Minister Yingluck Shinawatra, a coup, and the establishment of a military junta. The primary objective of the protests was to remove former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra and the establishment of an unelected "people's council" in Thailand.
options.B: The 2013–2014 Thai political crisis was a period of political instability in Thailand. Anti-government protests took place between November 2013 and May 2014, organised by the People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political pressure group led by former Democrat Party parliamentary representative (MP) Suthep Thaugsuban. The crisis eventually resulted in the removal of incumbent Prime Minister Yingluck Shinawatra, a coup d'état, and the establishment of a military junta. The primary aim of the protests was the removal of former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra's – the brother of Yingluck – influence on Thai politics and the creation of an unelected "people's council" to oversee reforms of the political system. Protesters viewed Thaksin as corrupt and damaging to Thailand's democracy, although he enjoyed strong support in many areas of Thailand,
options.C: A phase of political upheaval in Thailand, known as the 2013–2014 Thai political crisis, transpired during this time. Demonstrations against the government were organized by the People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political advocacy group spearheaded by Suthep Thaugsuban, a former Member of Parliament (MP) for the Democrat Party, and occurred between November 2013 and May 2014. This crisis ultimately culminated in the ousting of the then-current Prime Minister, Yingluck Shinawatra, a military takeover, and the subsequent formation of a military government. The main objective of these protests was to eradicate the influence of former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra – who was Yingluck's brother – from Thai politics and to establish an appointed "people's council" that would supervise modifications to the political framework. Despite enjoying considerable backing in numerous regions of Thailand, protesters perceived Thaksin as dishonest and detrimental to the democratic process in Thailand.
